title: Suregada rubra
intro: Suregada rubra, also known as the Red Ironwood or Red Suregada, is a tropical tree species native to the Indian Ocean region and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating fever, pain, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Suregada rubra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Suregada rubra is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Suregada rubra is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae, a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This family includes over 2,700 species of plants, many of which are known for their medicinal and economic importance. As a member of the Euphorbiaceae family, Suregada rubra is closely related to other plants in this group, such as the genus Euphorbia. The species is further classified within the genus Suregada, which is a group of plants native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The classification of Suregada rubra reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
